channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know on the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic collapsed nearly an hour after a merengue concert began, killing at least 98 people and injuring at least 160.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana man caught raping teen near White River
item.description: According to court documents filed on Sunday in Delaware County, 30-year-old Arijan Urguni has been preliminarily charged with:
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi exhibits include Richard Allen's confession tapes, police interviews
item.description: Allen would go on to make these confessions in several more of the released prison calls. These include calls made on June 5 and June 11, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Hearing could set rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 will argue some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a two-day hearing set to begin Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: American man on Bahamas family vacation found dead after leaving dinner to get jacket: report
item.description: An Maryland man, Dinari McAlmont, who briefly disappeared during a vacation to the Bahamas with his parents, was found dead on a beach the next morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin pauses $1B from Cornell, $790M from Northwestern amid civil rights investigation: report
item.description: The Trump admin is freezing $1 billion in federal funding from Cornell and $790 million from Northwestern University as both face civil rights investigations, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order seeking to bolster US coal mining
item.description: The Trump administration will also seek to exclude coal projects from environmental reviews and prioritize coal production on federal lands.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Texas college students reach across the aisle in an era of deep division
item.description: Both organizations have emerged against a backdrop of divisive politics and deep polarization throughout the country.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says New York must end Manhattan driving tolls this month
item.description: The Trump administration said Tuesday that it's not backing off its latest deadline for New York to end its $9 congestion toll on drivers entering the most traffic-snarled parts of Manhattan, despite a recent court filing that indicated the charge would remain at least through the summer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction to add more barriers along the U.S-Mexico border in Southern California even as illegal crossings have plummeted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Huntsman Cancer Institute breaks ground on new $400M Utah County cancer center
item.description: The Huntsman Cancer Institute broke ground on its new location in Utah County. Here's the project details and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Watch the world’s largest iceberg run aground near remote island in South Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Newly released satellite footage shows the world’s largest iceberg running aground near a remote island in the South Atlantic Ocean last month.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano spews fountains of lava in latest eruption episode
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Scientists were bracing for high lava fountains Tuesday during the latest episode of an ongoing eruption of Hawaii's Kilauea volcano.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cancels $188M in grants New York City was using to shelter migrants
item.description: The Trump administration canceled $188 million in federal grants meant to reimburse New York City for sheltering migrants, saying the money was being spent to support illegal immigration and leading the city's mayor to vow to fight the clawback.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former New Mexico college football player faces possible life in prison after drug trafficking conviction
item.description: A former college football player could be sentenced to life in prison for smuggling meth into a New Mexico jail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rejects new trials for 2 convicted of human smuggling in death of family of 4 from India
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday rejected requests for new trials for two men convicted on human smuggling charges in the deaths of four members of a family from India who froze to death while trying to cross the Canadian border into Minnesota during a blizzard in 2022. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill would prohibit Red 40 in foods sold in Tennessee schools
item.description: According to Rep. Davis, the bill would not apply to foods sold at concession stands, for school fundraisers, or foods available in vending machines on campuses.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: S&P 500 rises 3.4% as some relief washes through financial markets worldwide
item.description: Stocks are rising on Wall Street and around the world to recover some of the historic losses since last week when President Donald Trump announced sharp increases in taxes on goods imported to the U.S. The S&P 500 was up 3.4% in early trading Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man, 71, arrested after 7 'emotional support' tigers found in his house
item.description: A 71-year-old man from Nevada was arrested last week after authorities seized seven tigers, which he claimed were all “emotional support animals,” that he kept in his home, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KTLA 5

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks careened through a manic Monday after President Donald Trump threatened to crank his tariffs higher, despite a stunning display showing how dearly Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Step inside Universal's 'Epic' new theme park
item.description: Universal Orlando has revealed the first details of its highly anticipated new theme park, Epic Universe, which will officially open on May 22.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Stocks sink globally as Trump pushes ahead with trade war
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much financial markets would love to see him do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KRON4
